message:
fix: align installer and CI on gnu targets to match existing releases

The install.sh requested musl targets but all existing GitHub releases
ship gnu artifacts, causing 404 errors on every Linux installation
(both x86_64 and aarch64).

- install.sh: changed target from musl to gnu
- dist.yml: changed CI matrix from musl to gnu, removed musl-tools
  install step and static linking verification

This makes the installer, CI, and existing release artifacts all
consistent on gnu targets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -31,13 +31,13 @@ jobs:
       fail-fast: false
       matrix:
         include:
-          # Linux x86_64 (musl for static linking / portable binaries)
+          # Linux x86_64 (gnu to match existing release artifacts)
           - os: ubuntu-latest
-            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
+            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
             artifact_name: dcg
-          # Linux ARM64 (musl for static linking / portable binaries)
+          # Linux ARM64 (gnu to match existing release artifacts)
           - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
-            target: aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
+            target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
             artifact_name: dcg
           # macOS Intel
           - os: macos-15-intel
@@ -64,19 +64,9 @@ jobs:
         with:
           targets: ${{ matrix.target }}
 
-      - name: Install musl-tools (Linux musl builds)
-        if: contains(matrix.target, 'musl')
-        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y musl-tools
-
       - name: Build release binary
         run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }}
 
-      - name: Verify static linking (Linux musl)
-        if: contains(matrix.target, 'musl')
-        run: |
-          file target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ matrix.artifact_name }}
-          file target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ matrix.artifact_name }} | grep -qE "statically linked|static-pie linked"
-
       - name: Create tarball (Unix)
         if: runner.os != 'Windows'
         run: |
